title: mitrasacme paludosa
intro: Mitrasacme paludosa, also known as marsh mitrewort, is a plant species in the family Mitrasacmeaceae. It is native to Australia, where it grows in wetland areas and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine. The plant is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as eczema and acne. Mitrasacme paludosa can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and it has also been used in topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal benefits.
